hero.title: Agents propose. You approve. Every change on record.
hero.sub: NullDesk is a local app for running AI agents under human control: you assign structured tasks, review each proposed file change as a side-by-side diff, and approve or block shell commands before they run. Unless you explicitly put a task in its bounded autonomous mode, nothing touches disk until you accept, and every decision lands in an append-only log inside your workspace.

primary.label: Build from source (Zig 0.16.0)
primary.code:
git clone https://github.com/nullclaw/nulldesk
cd nulldesk
zig build

alts.label: Web frontend
alts.code:
zig build web
zig build run -- web --workspace "$PWD"

quickstart:
Build once, then point NullDesk at a workspace. Every frontend runs from the same binary.

```bash
# build the debug binary
zig build

# run the default frontend against the current directory
zig build run -- --workspace "$PWD"

# or pick a frontend explicitly
zig build run -- tui --workspace "$PWD"

# local web UI on 127.0.0.1:8765
zig build web
zig build run -- web --workspace "$PWD"
```

features.title: Review Center write boundary
features.body: Agents never write files directly. Every proposed change appears as a side-by-side diff you accept or reject — whole files or individual hunks. If the file changed on disk after the diff was made, accepting is refused instead of silently overwriting your work.

features.title: Risk-classified command approval
features.body: Shell commands an agent wants to run are classified low, medium, or high risk. High-risk patterns like curl|sh and rm -rf are shown but blocked from approval entirely.

features.title: Structured task packets
features.body: Tasks are JSON files under .nulldesk/tasks/ with goal, constraints, done criteria, budget, and timeout. Nine statuses track each one from Draft to Done or Failed.

features.title: Five autonomy modes
features.body: Each task sets how much its agent may do on its own, from ask_only (read and answer, nothing else) up to autonomous_within_limits — capped by default at 20 actions, $2.00 in cost (or the task budget, if lower), files under src/ and tests/ only, and no network or destructive commands.

features.title: Append-only audit timeline
features.body: Every task change, agent run, file read, review, approval, command, and commit lands in .nulldesk/events.jsonl. Decision history stays in the workspace, as plain files.

features.title: Three frontends, one core
features.body: A native macOS AppKit GUI, a cross-platform TUI, and a loopback-only web UI (SvelteKit SPA on 127.0.0.1:8765) compile from one shared Zig core into one binary.

note: Pre-release: no published releases, tags, or license file yet — build from source and expect change.

plain.what: A single Zig binary that opens a local project folder and gives you three frontends — a native macOS GUI, a cross-platform terminal UI, and a web UI served on 127.0.0.1 by default — for assigning work to AI agents (run through nullclaw, a small local agent runtime) and approving what they produce.
plain.why: Agents that edit files and run shell commands unsupervised are hard to trust, so NullDesk turns proposed file changes into reviewable diffs, requested commands into risk-classified approvals, and each decision into a line in an append-only audit log.
plain.when: When you want an AI agent to work on a repo or docs folder — fix a bug, write tests, draft docs — but you want to see and accept each diff and command yourself before anything lands on disk.
